backlogItem: B-052 (HDMI dashboard, US-257) + TD-041 (US-254) + TD-043 cleanup follow-up (US-255) + Sprint 19/20 retroactive validation gates (US-256, US-260, US-261) + B-047 self-update e2e drill (US-258)

testBaseline.note: Post-Sprint-20 close baseline. main@2800c6d (V0.20.1). Verify exact count at sprint start.

sprintNotes: GOAL: Sprint 19 fixed US-216 trigger source; Sprint 20 wired PowerMonitor to power_log; BUT 5/5 drain tests have STILL hard-crashed at the LiPo discharge knee because the part of US-216 that monitors VCELL during BATTERY state and fires staged shutdown NEVER ENGAGES. Sprint 21's lead story (US-252) finally closes this 5-drain-test architectural failure. Plus paired wake-on-power verification (US-253) so the post-graceful-shutdown -> wall-return -> auto-boot loop works end-to-end. Plus 8 more high-priority stories around TD cleanup (TD-041, TD-043), Sprint 19/20 acceptance-gap retros (US-256, US-260, US-261), self-update e2e drill (US-258), drain-test harness expansion (US-259), and HDMI dashboard full-canvas redesign (US-257 / B-052).

EXECUTION ORDER (deps-aware): TIER 1 no deps -- US-252, US-253, US-254, US-255, US-256, US-257, US-258, US-261. TIER 2 deps -- US-259 (deps US-252; integration test consumes the fix), US-260 (deps US-242/US-244 from Sprint 20 already shipped). 8 T1 + 2 T2.

THEME: 'The ladder finally fires.' Sprint 16-20 built every layer of the power-down system; Sprint 21 gets the actual safety feature working. Post-Sprint-21 + drain test 6: graceful shutdown event in journal, no EXT4 orphan cleanup, clean boot record. Plus the dashboard CIO sees during a drain finally fills the HDMI canvas with useful information.

CONSTRAINTS (per feedback_runtime_validation_required.md): synthetic tests for runtime-only-verifiable stories MUST tightly mock at hardware/lifecycle-signal level. US-252's synthetic test must mock VCELL stair-step from 4.20V down past 3.42V over mocked time AND mock the BATTERY-state entry path AND assert all stage transitions write rows to power_log AND assert subprocess.run(systemctl, poweroff) called exactly once at TRIGGER. Mocking high-level abstractions (e.g., directly calling Orchestrator.fireWarning) does NOT meet the bar -- the bug class that survived Sprint 19/20 was exactly this kind of 'unit tests passed against high-level mocks but full pipeline never engaged.' Live drills (drain test 6, wake-on-power drill, drive 6 cold-start) remain POST-SPRINT ACTION ITEMS per CIO no-human-tasks rule.

GROUNDING SOURCES: 2026-05-01 Spool drain-test-5 note (architectural-gap diagnosis with timeline + 3 hypotheses) + 2026-05-01 Spool manual-SQL-fix note (TD-043 + US-249 schema-diff retro motivation) + Sprint 19/20 closure docs + Sprint 20 deploy verification log + 5 drain tests of empirical VCELL/SOC trace data + B-052 backlog item.

POST-SPRINT-21 ACTION ITEMS (NOT in sprint.json per CIO rule): (1) CIO + Spool run drain test 6 with US-252+US-253 deployed; expect graceful shutdown event in journal + no EXT4 orphan cleanup + clean boot record. (2) CIO + Spool run wake-on-power drill standalone: sudo systemctl poweroff -> unplug -> wait 30s -> replug -> verify auto-boot within ~75s. (3) CIO + Spool run coupled drill: drain test -> graceful shutdown -> wall return -> auto-boot end-to-end. (4) CIO runs cold-start drive 6 with US-242 (idle-poll) + US-244 (BT-connect) + US-246 (defer-INSERT) deployed; verify drive_start fires within seconds + drive_summary metadata non-NULL + sync 200 OKs. (5) Marcus bumps deploy/RELEASE_VERSION at sprint close per feedback_pm_sprint_close_version_bump.md (V0.20.1 -> V0.21.0 expected; minor for new staged-shutdown firing path + new dashboard layout).

stories.title: US-216 staged shutdown actually fires -- wire VCELL-decline monitor to PowerMonitor stages
stories.intent: Across 5 drain tests over 3 weeks (Session 6 / Sprint 17 / Sprint 18 x2 / Sprint 20 post-deploy), the Pi has hard-crashed at the LiPo discharge knee (~3.36V). PowerMonitor correctly detects wall-out + writes transition rows to power_log (Sprint 20 US-243 ship), and US-234's VCELL thresholds are correctly configured (3.70V / 3.55V / 3.45V from Sprint 19), but the staged-shutdown stages NEVER ENGAGE: zero rows in power_log for WARNING / IMMINENT / TRIGGER stages, no systemctl poweroff event in journal, EXT4 orphan-cleanup smoking gun on every boot. Spool's 3 hypotheses (need Rex audit to distinguish): (A) staged-shutdown logic written but never subscribed to PowerMonitor's new callback chain; (B) VCELL polling happens but threshold-comparison logic is in a different module not subscribed to the polling loop; (C) thresholds are checked but the action ladder (systemctl poweroff at TRIGGER) calls into a still-dead path. After this story, drain test 6 should produce stage-transition rows in power_log + clean systemctl poweroff event + zero EXT4 orphan cleanup on next boot.

stories.completionNotes: Hypothesis (A) MATCHED.  PowerDownOrchestrator IS instantiated (HardwareManager._initializePowerDownOrchestrator at hardware_manager.py:325) AND its callbacks ARE wired (lifecycle._wirePowerDownOrchestratorCallbacks attaches onWarning/onImminent/onAcRestore post-construction).  But tick() was the dead path: only caller was HardwareManager._displayUpdateLoop, which only spawned when StatusDisplay successfully initialized.  Any pygame fault, missing HDMI cable, or pi.hardware.statusDisplay.enabled=false silently disabled the safety ladder.  FIX: dedicated HardwareManager._powerDownTickLoop running on its own daemon thread spawned whenever upsMonitor + powerDownOrchestrator are wired, regardless of display state.  Plus power_log forensic trail: new vcell REAL column (idempotent ensurePowerLogVcellColumn migration mirroring ensureDataSourceColumn pattern) + 3 new event_type values (stage_warning/stage_imminent/stage_trigger) + powerLogWriter callable injected through orchestrator constructor (closure built in lifecycle._createPowerLogWriter, mirrors _createBatteryHealthRecorder).  6/6 new tests pass; 198 passed/11 skipped in adjacent regression (tests/pi/power/ + tests/pi/hardware/ + tests/pi/integration/ -m 'not slow').  Discriminator: TestPowerDownTickThreadDecoupledFromDisplay::test_tick_loop_runs_without_status_display would FAIL pre-fix because tick() never fires when _statusDisplay is None.  TestStagedShutdownActuallyFires::test_drain_4_20_to_3_40_writes_three_stage_rows_to_power_log would FAIL pre-fix because orchestrator has no powerLogWriter parameter.

Sprint-19/20 PowerMonitor wiring (US-243) was already correct -- power_log gets transition rows on AC<->BATTERY changes.  Sprint 19 US-235 + Sprint 19 US-234 also unchanged -- the bug was always tick() lifecycle, not state-machine logic.

stories.title: Wake-on-power verification + deploy-pi.sh POWER_OFF_ON_HALT enforcement
stories.intent: Post-B-043-wiring production scenario: key-OFF removes wall power, US-216 fires graceful shutdown, Pi halts; key-ON returns wall power and Pi MUST auto-boot without operator intervention. Pi 5 bootloader controls this via POWER_OFF_ON_HALT EEPROM config (0=auto-wake on power return; 1=deep sleep, requires button or full power-cycle). Per Spool 2026-05-01 audit: chi-eclipse-01 has the right setting (POWER_OFF_ON_HALT not set -> defaults to 0). But this has never been tested end-to-end because all 5 drain tests crashed before reaching graceful poweroff state. Risk: an eeprom-config edit out-of-band could silently flip the setting. Fix: deploy-pi.sh enforces the correct setting on every deploy + adds a synthetic test that the deploy script's config-write step is correct. Wake-on-power drill itself is a post-sprint action item.

filesToTouch: deploy/deploy-pi.sh (NEW step: verify-or-set EEPROM POWER_OFF_ON_HALT=0 via rpi-eeprom-config; idempotent; warn if mismatch and write only if needed)

tests/deploy/test_deploy_pi_eeprom_config.py (NEW -- mocks rpi-eeprom-config command output; asserts deploy script invokes the right rpi-eeprom-config commands when setting differs; asserts no-op when setting already correct)
specs/architecture.md (NEW subsection -- POWER_OFF_ON_HALT contract + production wake-on-power loop)
deploy/README.md (UPDATE -- document the EEPROM config enforcement step)

stories.completionNotes: Sprint 21 2/10 -- wake-on-power EEPROM enforcement live on every deploy.  Standalone enforcement-script pattern (deploy/enforce-eeprom-power-off-on-halt.sh) decouples the production logic from deploy-pi.sh, unlocking PATH-mocked rpi-eeprom-config testing on the dev workstation across all 7 real-world states (absent / =0 / =1 / =2 / tool missing / apply fails / two-run idempotency).  The enforcement step runs idempotently in deploy-pi.sh's default + --init flows AFTER sync_tree (parallels step_install_journald_persistent and step_install_eclipse_obd_unit hygiene-on-every-deploy pattern); --dry-run mode prints what would happen without touching the Pi.  Mock seam: $RPI_EEPROM_CONFIG env var override that defaults to plain `rpi-eeprom-config` PATH lookup -- production callers never set it.  Discriminator: test_eeprom_power_off_on_halt.sh scenario 7 (idempotency drill) would FAIL pre-fix because no enforcement script existed; scenario 3 (POWER_OFF_ON_HALT=1 -> 0 rewrite) proves the production-script catches the bug class (bootloader setting silently flipped non-zero by an out-of-band rpi-eeprom-config --edit).  Real end-to-end drill (graceful poweroff -> unplug -> wait -> replug -> verify auto-boot) remains a post-sprint mechanical CIO action item per no-human-tasks rule -- code-side this story makes that drill possible to ship by guaranteeing the EEPROM setting is correct on every deploy.
stories.scopeFenceNote: Story scope.filesToTouch listed (1) deploy/deploy-pi.sh -- TOUCHED (added step_enforce_eeprom_power_off_on_halt + wired into default-mode body after step_install_journald_persistent + updated top-of-file documentation); (2) tests/deploy/test_deploy_pi_eeprom_config.py -- CREATED; (3) specs/architecture.md -- TOUCHED (added 'Wake-on-Power EEPROM Contract (US-253, Sprint 21)' subsection at end of Section 11 Deployment Architecture + Modification History entry); (4) deploy/README.md -- TOUCHED (Files table + new 'Wake-on-power EEPROM enforcement (US-253)' subsection).  ADDITIONAL FILES introduced beyond literal scope, structurally within story intent: (a) deploy/enforce-eeprom-power-off-on-halt.sh NEW -- the standalone enforcement script the test harness exercises; story scope spoke of 'deploy-pi.sh adds a step that reads current rpi-eeprom-config' but a step within deploy-pi.sh that SSH-runs sub-bash strings through `ssh ... 'sudo ...'` is not test-mockable from the workstation (subprocess.run('ssh', ...) only sees the wrapper exit code, not the inner mock).  Extracting the body to a standalone script is the canonical pattern for making PATH-mock testing work and mirrors deploy/install-rfcomm-bind.sh (US-196) + deploy/journald-persistent.conf (US-210) which both ship as standalone artifacts that deploy-pi.sh stages on the Pi via rsync and then invokes via ssh.  (b) tests/deploy/test_eeprom_power_off_on_halt.sh NEW -- the canonical assertion catalog the python wrapper drives via subprocess; mirrors test_deploy_pi.sh + test_journald_persistent_install.sh pattern.  Per Refusal Rule #3 these adjacent additions are within the story acceptance contract ('mocks rpi-eeprom-config returning various states; assert deploy invokes correct subprocess commands when setting differs; assert no-op when setting already correct' -- the test harness shape is the load-bearing part).

feedback.grounding: Spool's 2026-05-01 drain-test-5 inbox note Section 6 ('wake-on-power verification') established the load-bearing facts: POWER_OFF_ON_HALT=0 (or absent, default 0) keeps the PMIC awake watching power rails after `systemctl poweroff` so the Pi auto-wakes when wall power returns; non-zero puts the board in deep sleep requiring a button press.  Spool confirmed via `sudo rpi-eeprom-config` on chi-eclipse-01 that the setting is currently absent (correct default).  CIO 2026-05-01 raised the production-scenario question: post-B-043-wiring there is no operator at the car to press a button.  The risk vector (drift-prevention rule, per groundingRefs source 'Spool drain-test-5 note Section 6'): an `rpi-eeprom-config --edit` for an unrelated reason can flip the setting silently, breaking the wake-on-power loop only at the next wall-power transition (i.e. during a real drill, not during deploy).  Hence the deploy-time enforcement design.  Per groundingRefs source 'feedback_sprint_scope_dev_only.md' the actual physical drill (unplug/replug/verify auto-boot) is a post-sprint mechanical action item -- this story ships the code-side guarantee that the EEPROM setting is correct on every deploy, which is the precondition that drill needs.  Hypothesis-tree analogue to US-252 not applicable here because there's no Hypothesis A/B/C bug class to investigate -- this is greenfield enforcement of an existing-correct setting.  Pre-flight audit method: rg `rpi-eeprom-config|POWER_OFF_ON_HALT|EEPROM` deploy/ src/ tests/ returned only PM-state files (sprint.json, progress.txt, ralph_agents.json, story_counter.json) and the Spool inbox note that motivated the story -- zero live deploy or test references; greenfield.

stories.title: TD-041 close: PowerMonitor.getStatus non-reentrant lock deadlock fix
stories.intent: Per TD-041 (filed Sprint 20 US-243 verification): PowerMonitor.getStatus uses a non-reentrant lock that can deadlock if called from inside a callback fired from within the locked section. Low likelihood today but becomes a hazard post-US-252 when staged-shutdown logic adds another consumer of getStatus. Fix: convert to threading.RLock OR refactor getStatus to read state without locking (snapshot read).

filesToTouch: src/pi/power/power_monitor.py (PowerMonitor -- replace threading.Lock with RLock OR refactor getStatus to read without locking; add docstring documenting choice)
tests/pi/power/test_power_monitor_db_write.py (UPDATE -- add test calling getStatus from within callback fired from locked section; asserts no deadlock)
offices/pm/tech_debt/TD-041-powermonitor-getstatus-non-reentrant-lock-deadlock.md (UPDATE -- mark resolved)

groundingRefs.value: Non-reentrant Lock blocks if same thread re-acquires; deadlocks orchestrator under specific callback ordering
Sprint 21 US-252 will add another getStatus consumer (staged-shutdown ladder) -- raises deadlock probability
Two valid fixes: RLock OR lockless snapshot read

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg `Lock|RLock|getStatus|handleTransition` src/pi/power/power_monitor.py.
PowerMonitor.getStatus no longer deadlocks when called from a callback fired within handleTransition
Existing tests pass + new test demonstrates the deadlock is fixed
Docstring updated explaining locking approach + why
TD-041 marked resolved
Fast suite + new tests pass; ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors
invariants: Public API unchanged (handleTransition, getStatus, getCurrentPowerState)
Thread-safety preserved
Performance comparable
stopConditions: If deadlock is actually caused by ordering bug not lock kind -- STOP, document actual race
If lockless read can't guarantee multi-step coherence -- STOP, default to RLock
stories.completionNotes: Sprint 21 3/10 -- TD-041 closed.  Chose Option (a) RLock over Option (b) snapshot-outside refactor.  Initial attempt with Option (b) (capture self.getStats() snapshot before outer `with self._lock`) PASSED the direct-call test but FAILED the canonical story-scoped scenario test (getStatus called from inside checkPowerStatus()'s locked-section callback) -- when the producer thread already holds _lock, the inner getStats() re-acquire deadlocks regardless of where in getStatus() the call lives.  Only RLock addresses both shapes.  Audit of all 6 `with self._lock` sites confirmed only getStatus composed a nested acquire; start/stop/checkPowerStatus/getStats/resetStats are leaf acquirers and unaffected.  Discriminator test: TestGetStatusNoDeadlock::test_getStatus_calledFromInsideCallback_doesNotDeadlock would FAIL with Option (b) (or pre-fix); PASS with RLock.  Both new tests use threading.Thread + join(timeout=2.0) so a deadlock surfaces as a hard 2s timeout rather than hanging the pytest run.
stories.scopeFenceNote: Story scope.filesToTouch listed `src/pi/power/power_monitor.py` -- 8TH SESSION IN A ROW HITTING STORY-SCOPE PHANTOM-PATH DRIFT (Sessions 114-122 noted similar; Session 123 + 124 + 125 also).  Actual file lives at `src/pi/power/power.py` (refactored from power_monitor.py back in US-012, 2026-01-22 per its own mod-history line 12).  TD-041 itself uses the correct path (src/pi/power/power.py:762-783) but the sprint.json story scope does not.  The PRD-grooming/template generator that normalizes paths to `src/pi/<module>/<module>_monitor.py` instead of consulting actual repo layout is the upstream root cause -- this is a Marcus-side template fix not a Ralph-side fix.  Audited at the actual location.  No additional files outside the literal scope were touched.

feedback.grounding: TD-041 (filed Session 115, US-243 work) traced the deadlock to `_lock = threading.Lock()` at power.py:180 (non-reentrant) + getStatus at line 762 acquiring _lock then calling self.getStats() at line 782 which also acquires the same _lock.  Empirical reproduction: TestGetStatusNoDeadlock::test_getStatus_doesNotDeadlock_returnsWithinTimeout (a fresh PowerMonitor + getStatus call on a worker thread) hung indefinitely pre-fix, surfaced as 2s timeout assertion.  Story acceptance broadened the scope: 'PowerMonitor.getStatus no longer deadlocks when called from a callback fired within handleTransition'.  Empirical reproduction of the broader case: TestGetStatusNoDeadlock::test_getStatus_calledFromInsideCallback_doesNotDeadlock (registers an onReading callback that calls getStatus, then triggers checkPowerStatus -- the callback fires from inside checkPowerStatus's `with self._lock` block) also hung pre-fix.  Pre-flight audit: rg `Lock|RLock|getStatus|handleTransition` src/pi/power/power.py confirmed only getStatus composed a nested-acquire across the 6 `with self._lock` sites.  Post-fix verification: 9/9 PASS in target test file (7 existing + 2 new); 200 passed / 11 skipped / 0 regressions across tests/pi/power/ + tests/pi/hardware/ + tests/pi/integration/ -m 'not slow'.

stories.title: v0007 migration: drop legacy drive_summary columns (TD-043 follow-up)
stories.intent: Per Spool 2026-05-01 manual-SQL-fix note Section 2 question 2 and TD-043 Sprint 21 follow-up: Sprint 19 US-237 v0004 migration ALTERed in modern Pi-sync columns but kept the 6 legacy Sprint-7/8 columns (device_id, start_time, end_time, duration_seconds, profile_id, row_count) which are now orphaned. Sprint 21 v0006 migration made them nullable. Time to fully remove. Audit FIRST: rg every reference to those 6 column names; verify zero live consumers in src/ or tests/. Then write v0007 migration that DROPs the columns.

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg `device_id|start_time|end_time|duration_seconds|profile_id|row_count` src/server/ tests/server/ scripts/. Classify EVERY reference as (a) legacy-column-to-drop OR (b) different context.
If audit finds live consumers in (a): STOP, file follow-up story; do not proceed with DROP
If audit clean: write v0007 migration mirroring v0006 pattern + register in ALL_MIGRATIONS
Migration is idempotent: probe each column, DROP only if present

Each column dropped independently (one ALTER with multiple DROP COLUMN clauses, one transaction)

v0001-v0006 migrations untouched
stopConditions: If audit finds ANY live consumer of the 6 legacy columns -- STOP IMMEDIATELY
If MariaDB rejects multi-DROP transaction -- STOP, fall back to one-DROP-per-statement
stories.completionNotes: STOPPED on stopConditions[0] -- pre-flight audit found 11 live consumers of the 6 'legacy' drive_summary columns across src/server/services/analysis.py (active writer on every drive-end auto-analysis trigger; modern + legacy paths both populate all 6), src/server/analytics/calibration.py (filter on device_id from --calibrate CLI), src/server/analytics/advanced.py (start_time ordering for trend analysis), src/server/reports/drive_report.py (start_time ordering + date-range queries from CIO drive-report CLI), scripts/load_data.py + scripts/reconcile_drive_summary.py + scripts/spool_prompt_invoke.py + 3 tests + the DriveSummary ORM model itself.  The DriveSummary model docstring (src/server/db/models.py:547-572) explicitly documents this: 'Analytics path... finds the existing Pi-sync row by (source_device, drive_id) and UPDATEs the analytics fields in place'.  The 6 columns ARE the analytics-writer's column set -- they're not Pi-side orphaned, they're the second writer's column group.  Spool's grooming hypothesis ('the 6 legacy columns are now orphaned by the schema modernization') was understandable from the Pi perspective (Pi doesn't write them) but incorrect.  v0006 (already shipped, made them nullable) IS the correct fix -- it lets Pi-sync INSERT without populating analytics-only columns.  Dropping them would require Option B's cross-cutting refactor of analytics + reports + 2 CLIs, conservatively L size, with two production code paths (auto-analysis on sync trigger + drive_report CLI) that need live-drill verification per feedback_runtime_validation_required.md.  Recommended path forward (Option A): close TD-043 Sprint 21 follow-up as won't-fix, update DriveSummary docstring to drop the 'legacy' framing.  All audit details + classification + 3 resolution options in offices/pm/blockers/BL-008.md.
stories.scopeFenceNote: Audit-only completion -- no production code, ORM model, or migration was touched.  Stayed strictly within scope.filesToRead per Refusal Rule #3 (Scope Fence) + stopConditions[0].  No v0007 migration created, no ORM edits, no test edits, no TD-043 markdown changes.

feedback.grounding: BL-008 audit found 6 "legacy" columns are LIVE analytics-writer columns (11 consumers). Option A approved by Marcus 2026-05-01. v0006 nullable fix (Sprint 21 grooming) is the correct + complete remediation. No v0007 migration needed. Optional docstring rename of "legacy" -> "analytics" can roll into US-256 retro.

stories.title: Sprint 19/20 retro: integration test catches TD-043 class + strengthen US-249 schema-diff
stories.intent: Sprint 19 US-237 and Sprint 20 US-249 (schema-diff CLI) both shipped passes:true but missed TD-043 (drive_summary device_id NOT NULL blocked Pi sync for weeks). Two-part fix: (a) integration test that fails against pre-TD-043-fix code -- mocks Pi-shaped INSERT against modernized drive_summary schema and verifies success; (b) strengthen US-249's scripts/schema_diff.py to detect NOT-NULL-without-default columns on the server side that aren't populated by Pi-sync writer. Direct application of feedback_runtime_validation_required.md.

stories.completionNotes: Sprint 21 4/10 (was 3/10 + US-255 BL-008-blocked).  Sprint 19/20 retro complete: integration test locks in TD-043 bug class + schema_diff.py gains TD-043 detection rule.  PRE-FLIGHT AUDIT (rg drive_summary tests/server/) found 3 sites: test_drive_summary_sync_post_reconcile.py is the closest pattern (ephemeral SQLite + Pi-shape payload + assert success) but did NOT exercise the pre-v0006 NOT-NULL trap because Base.metadata.create_all builds the modern (nullable) ORM shape.  Gap closed by building pre-v0006 schema via raw DDL.  ARCHITECTURE: ephemeral SQLite + raw DDL for the legacy table + ORM-driven create_all for sibling tables (sync_history etc.) so the upsert handler's foreign refs resolve normally.  Discriminator pair: TestPreV0006FailureMode (2 tests) asserts IntegrityError/OperationalError surfaces on the pre-v0006 shape; TestPostV0006Modernized (2 tests) asserts the modern ORM shape accepts the same payload.  If v0006 ever rolls back, the pre-v0006 test passes silently AND the post-v0006 test fires loudly -- bug class caught either direction.  SCHEMA-DIFF EXTENSION: new loadServerNotNullNoDefault() inspects ORM table metadata for column.nullable=False AND column.default=None AND column.server_default=None (auto-increment integer PKs filtered out so the rule doesn't fire on by-design id columns).  computeDiff() gains an optional serverNotNullNoDefault kwarg; when provided, computes serverRequiredColumnsMissingOnPi (filtered by SERVER_MIRROR_COLUMNS so source_id/source_device/synced_at/sync_batch_id NOT-NULL declarations don't trip the rule).  Back-compat: 2-arg callers see no new keys in the output dict.  CLI main() trips exit-1 on EITHER TD-039 (Pi-only drift) OR TD-043 (server-required-Pi-omits) gate.  METHODOLOGY SPEC: new 'Integration Tests for Runtime-Verifiable Bugs' subsection under Section 4 (TDD), 3 exemplars cited (TD-043 retro / US-216 retro / schema-diff strengthening) + 4-step pattern checklist (lowest-mock-boundary, reproduce-failure-shape, pre-fix-FAILS + post-fix-PASSES discriminator, docstring-documents-the-catch).  Direct application of feedback_runtime_validation_required.md.  VERIFICATION: pytest tests/server/test_drive_summary_pi_shape_insert.py tests/scripts/test_schema_diff.py 36/36 PASS in 60.72s; ruff clean on touched files; python scripts/schema_diff.py runs green (tablesWithRequiredColumnGap=[] -- no false positives on current schema); python scripts/schema_diff.py exit 0 on current; sprint_lint 0 errors / 21 warnings (matches Session 127 baseline); FULL FAST SUITE 3663 passed/17 skipped/19 deselected/26 failed in 880.41s.  Math: 3650 baseline (Session 126 final 3646 + US-253 Python tests + US-254 tests) + 13 new US-256 tests = 3663. ZERO US-256 regressions; all 24 unique failures pre-existing TD-042 + TD-044 (16 in tests/pi/update/* TD-042 + 7 in tests/server/test_release_* TD-042 + 1 in tests/server/test_migration_0005_dtc_log::test_appendedAtEnd TD-044).  validate_config.py OK.  PROMISE TAG: PARTIAL_BLOCKED.  Sprint 21 has 5 unblocked stories remaining (US-257, US-258, US-259, US-260, US-261).

stories.title: HDMI dashboard full-canvas redesign (B-052)
stories.intent: Per CIO 2026-05-01: dashboard renders 480x320 (legacy Pi touchscreen footprint) on the Eclipse's HDMI display, occupying only a small fraction of the screen canvas. Goal: redesign layout to use full HDMI canvas (1920x1080 or 1280x720) with larger fonts + clearer state indicators visible at driving distance. Surfaces all Sprint 16+ states + post-US-252 shutdown-stage with appropriate color coding. Backwards compat: 480x320 layout still works (config-selectable) for dev/testing.

filesToTouch: src/pi/hardware/status_display.py (refactor layout calculation to be canvas-size-aware; current hardcoded 480x320 positions become functions of (width, height))
src/pi/hardware/dashboard_layout.py (NEW or refactor -- 4-quadrant layout: engine NW, power NE, drive SW, alerts SE)
config.json display: add displayCanvas: {width: 1920, height: 1080, mode: 'auto'}
src/common/config/validator.py DEFAULTS for new keys
tests/pi/hardware/test_dashboard_layout.py (NEW -- mocks pygame.Surface various sizes; asserts each quadrant rendered correctly; asserts text fits within bounds)
tests/pi/hardware/test_status_display.py (UPDATE -- parameterize over canvas sizes; existing 480x320 tests still pass)
specs/architecture.md (UPDATE -- dashboard section)
offices/pm/backlog/B-052-hdmi-dashboard-full-canvas-redesign.md (UPDATE -- mark resolved)

groundingRefs.value: Current dashboard 480x320; HDMI canvas typically 1280x720 or 1920x1080
4-quadrant layout (engine NW / power NE / drive SW / alerts SE)
Color palette: NORMAL=green, WARNING=amber, IMMINENT=orange, TRIGGER=red
Backwards compat: 480x320 dev/test layout preserved

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg `480|320|set_mode|status_display` src/pi/hardware/.
config.json display.displayCanvas section added (width, height, mode='auto')
StatusDisplay layout accepts (canvas_width, canvas_height) and computes quadrant rects proportionally
Each quadrant renders dedicated state surface (engine / power / drive / alerts)
Power quadrant surfaces shutdown-stage color (NORMAL=green, WARNING=amber, IMMINENT=orange, TRIGGER=red)
Synthetic test: mock pygame.Surface at 1920x1080 + 1280x720 + 480x320; assert layout positions; assert text fits
Existing test_status_display.py tests still pass
Fast suite + new tests pass; ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors
invariants: Layout proportional, not fixed-pixel -- works at any canvas size
Quadrant assignments fixed for muscle memory
Color coding fixed for accessibility
Backwards compat: 480x320 mode renders without regression
stopConditions: If pygame text rendering at large sizes is significantly slower -- STOP after one optimization attempt
If pygame.display.Info auto-detect unreliable on production Pi -- STOP, default to explicit config
If 4-quadrant layout doesn't fit all state surfaces -- STOP, propose different layout in feedback note
stories.completionNotes: Pre-flight audit confirmed DISPLAY_WIDTH/DISPLAY_HEIGHT/FONT_SIZE_*/LINE_HEIGHT_*/PADDING were ALL module-private to src/pi/hardware/status_display.py -- no external imports.  HardwareManager._initializeStatusDisplay (hardware_manager.py:417) constructs StatusDisplay(refreshRate=..., forceSoftwareRenderer=...) WITHOUT width/height, so default args drove the legacy 480x320 footprint and a constructor-only refactor was safe.  Pi has TWO display surfaces (per architecture.md Â§10 "Two Display Surfaces"): pi.display.* (primary driving screen, x11 native renderer) and pi.hardware.status_display (overlay, SDL software renderer per US-198 / TD-024) -- the story's scope was exclusively the latter.  ARCHITECTURE: extracted a pure-geometry module pi.hardware.dashboard_layout with a frozen DashboardLayout dataclass (4 quadrant Rects + footer Rect + FontScale + padding); computeLayout(width, height) is the single factory.  Quadrant assignments fixed for muscle memory (NW=engine / NE=power / SW=drive / SE=alerts) per B-052 spec.  Font scaling = linear in canvasHeight against a 1080-tall reference, clamped to a readable minimum (title>=20 / value>=18 / label>=16 / detail>=14) so the legacy 480x320 case still renders without truncation.  STAGED-SHUTDOWN BANNER: ShutdownStage enum (NORMAL/WARNING/IMMINENT/TRIGGER) with the stage->color palette (NORMAL=green / WARNING=amber / IMMINENT=orange / TRIGGER=red).  StatusDisplay.updateShutdownStage(stage) accepts both the enum and its string value with case-insensitive coercion + unknown-string-coerces-to-NORMAL.  NE quadrant background tints to a 1/3-intensity stage color during transitions (NORMAL stays black to avoid alarm fatigue) so an operator several feet from the screen reads the stage at a glance.  CONFIG SURFACE: additive pi.display.displayCanvas.{width:1920, height:1080, mode:'auto'} with matching validator DEFAULTS.  Legacy pi.display.width/height keys unchanged for dev/test backwards compat.  TEST SURFACE: 27 tests in tests/pi/hardware/test_dashboard_layout.py (pure-geometry parametrized over 1920x1080 / 1280x720 / 480x320; quadrants tile canvas-minus-footer with no gaps + no overlaps + no zero-dim quadrants; font hierarchy ordered title>=value>=label>=detail; padding scales; stage colors distinct + RGB-valid; input validation raises ValueError on non-positive dims) + 13 new canvas-size + shutdown-stage tests in tests/pi/hardware/test_status_display.py exercising the full render path under fakePygame fixture (asserts pygame.display.flip is called and pygame.display.set_mode receives the constructor canvas size).  All 51 layout/display tests pass clean; adjacent tests/test_obd_config_loader.py + tests/pi/hardware/test_manager_status_display_disable.py (93 tests total) also pass.  ruff check src/pi/hardware/ tests/pi/hardware/ src/common/config/validator.py = All checks passed.  python validate_config.py = All validations passed.  python offices/pm/scripts/sprint_lint.py = 0 errors.  FAST SUITE caveat: pytest tests/ -m 'not slow' shows 3703 passed / 26 PRE-EXISTING failures (all in scripts/seed_eclipse_vin.py B-044 audit + Sprint-20 update + Sprint-19/20 server release/migration tests -- files I did NOT touch; sample failure: B-044 audit fingers seed_eclipse_vin.py:80,332,333 from commit 35e1374, not US-257 territory).

All 8 touched as planned, no scope expansion.  No tangential fixes; no other source files edited.  The 26 pre-existing fast-suite failures (B-044 audit on seed_eclipse_vin.py, Sprint-20 update apply/check tests, server release/migration tests) are in files outside scope.filesToTouch and were already failing before US-257 started -- per Scope Fence rule #3 + drift-observation rule, NOT touched in this story.  Marcus may wrap them into Sprint 22 stories or assign to other sprint slots.

feedback.grounding: B-052 backlog item Open Design Questions (CIO 2026-05-01 drain-test-5 observation 'the display is way too small for what the HDMI screen is') established the 4-quadrant layout (Spool design proposal) + the (NORMAL=green / WARNING=amber / IMMINENT=orange / TRIGGER=red) palette + the backwards-compat 480x320 invariant.  Story acceptance criterion #3 ('StatusDisplay layout accepts (canvas_width, canvas_height) and computes quadrant rects proportionally') drove the pure-geometry computeLayout(w,h) factory.  Acceptance criterion #5 ('Power quadrant surfaces shutdown-stage color') drove the ShutdownStage enum + STAGE_COLORS palette + updateShutdownStage setter on StatusDisplay.  Acceptance criterion #6 ('mock pygame.Surface at 1920x1080 + 1280x720 + 480x320; assert layout positions; assert text fits') drove the parametrize triple in test_dashboard_layout.py.  Acceptance criterion #7 ('Existing test_status_display.py tests still pass') drove preserving the SDL-hint contract verbatim in the refactored status_display.py -- fakePygame fixture continues to test _initializePygame's env-hint-before-init contract.  groundingRefs[3] (config-driven canvas dims, NOT pygame.display.Info auto-detection at runtime) recommended explicit displayCanvas.width/height with mode='auto' as a hint, not a behavior change.

stories.title: B-047 US-E: Pi self-update e2e drill -- synthetic apply path test
stories.intent: Sprint 20 shipped B-047 US-A through US-D. Apply path has unit-test coverage but no e2e drill exercising full check -> marker -> apply -> restart -> verify cycle. This story adds synthetic e2e: mocks server release endpoint returning NEWER version; runs UpdateChecker; observes marker file written; runs UpdateApplier (applyEnabled=true in test); mocks deploy-pi.sh + git fetch; observes apply succeed; observes post-deploy verification reading new .deploy-version. Required to confidently flip applyEnabled=true in production.

filesToTouch: tests/pi/integration/test_self_update_e2e.py (NEW -- full check->marker->apply->verify cycle; mocks server release endpoint + deploy-pi.sh subprocess + git fetch)
tests/pi/integration/conftest.py (UPDATE if needed -- shared fixtures)
specs/architecture.md (UPDATE -- self-update lifecycle diagram)

groundingRefs.value: B-047 US-E is e2e gate before flipping applyEnabled=true in production
Mocks at HTTP server boundary + subprocess boundary -- not at internal-state level
Drive-state-is-sacred: e2e drill must include test that apply skips during active drive

acceptance: Pre-flight audit: rg `update_checker|update_applier` tests/.
tests/pi/integration/test_self_update_e2e.py: full integration test with mocked HTTP server + subprocess
Test 1: server NEWER version -> UpdateChecker writes marker -> UpdateApplier reads -> mocks --dry-run success -> mocks apply success -> verify new .deploy-version reflects target -> marker deleted
Test 2: apply runs -> mocks deploy-pi.sh failure -> verify rollback path -> marker deleted -> log shows rollback
Test 3: drive_state=DRIVING -> verify update-check skipped + apply skipped -> marker not written
Test 4: server SAME version -> verify no marker + no apply attempted
Fast suite + new tests pass; ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors
invariants: Test exercises real production code path -- mocks only at HTTP + subprocess boundaries
No production-code changes
Test runs in <10s
stopConditions: If e2e drill reveals bugs in update_checker or update_applier -- STOP, file specific TDs
If marker/staging path requires sudo -- STOP, propose user-writable path

stories.completionNotes: E2E integration drill (7 tests / 5 classes) for B-047 self-update pipeline. Real UpdateChecker (US-247) + UpdateApplier (US-248) drive every assertion -- mocks ONLY at HTTP boundary (httpOpener) + subprocess boundary (subprocessRun). TestSelfUpdateE2EHappyPath: server NEWER -> check writes marker -> apply runs priorRef + fetch + checkout + dry-run + deploy + post-verify in order -> .deploy-version reflects target -> marker cleared -> SUCCESS. TestSelfUpdateE2EDeployFailureTriggersRollback (x2): full-deploy failure fires rollback chain (git checkout priorRef + systemctl restart eclipse-obd) + clears marker; dry-run failure short-circuits BEFORE full deploy fires. TestSelfUpdateE2EDriveStateGate: stale marker survives the drive (deferred-apply convention); checker skips HTTP request; applier spawns ZERO subprocesses. TestSelfUpdateE2EUpToDate: server SAME version -> no marker -> apply NO_MARKER + ZERO subprocesses. TestSelfUpdateE2EWireShape: X-API-Key header + GET /api/v1/release/current invariant audit through the integrated path. Architectural decisions: (a) _FailFullDeployRunner subclass discriminates the full deploy command (2-arg argv) from the dry-run (3-arg argv) by argv length because substring matching cannot cleanly separate them; (b) _FakeDeployRunner simulates the deploy-pi.sh .deploy-version stamp side-effect when the full deploy command runs (NOT --dry-run) so the post-deploy verify step reads the expected shape. specs/architecture.md Section 11 gained "Pi Self-Update Lifecycle (B-047 US-A through US-E, Sprints 19-21)" subsection with the two-process pipeline ASCII diagram, safety-gate ordering rationale, priorRef-and-rollback contract, and the e2e drill coverage table. Pre-existing TD-042 already documents the broader theme-field-broke-unit-tests breakage (24 unit tests in tests/pi/update/ + tests/server/) -- US-258 fixture was authored against the current (post-theme) contract so my 7 e2e tests pass cleanly even with TD-042 unresolved. Verification: pytest 7/7 pass (13.49s); pytest tests/pi/integration/ 47 passed / 7 skipped (55.95s); ruff clean; sprint_lint 0 errors. This drill is the integration-readiness gate before flipping pi.update.applyEnabled=true in production.
stories.scopeFenceNote: Files touched match scope.filesToTouch exactly: tests/pi/integration/test_self_update_e2e.py (NEW) + specs/architecture.md (UPDATE). conftest.py was NOT updated (no shared fixtures needed beyond the standard tmp_path / monkeypatch / caplog already provided by tests/conftest.py at the repo root). doNotTouch boundary held: zero production-code edits in src/pi/update/ or deploy/deploy-pi.sh. TD-042 acknowledged as out-of-scope; my fixture uses theme correctly without touching the broken upstream tests.

stories.title: Drain-test e2e harness expansion -- assert stage-firing + graceful poweroff
stories.intent: Sprint 20 US-245 shipped a power-mgmt e2e integration test. It mocked the pipeline at I2cClient.readWord level + asserted stage transitions. But drain test 5 still failed in production -- evidence the test wasn't exercising the same code path. Post-US-252 fix, this story expands the US-245 harness to be a high-fidelity end-to-end test that mirrors what drain test 6 would observe. After this + US-252, a passing test_power_mgmt_e2e_drain.py is high-confidence ground-truth for drain test 6.

tests/pi/integration/test_power_mgmt_lifecycle.py (NEW -- companion test exercising FULL lifecycle: startup -> idle EXTERNAL -> wall-out -> BATTERY -> stage-firing -> systemctl poweroff -> [boot replay] -> service restart -> idle EXTERNAL)

stories.title: Drive 6 cold-start lifecycle synthetic test (US-242 + US-244 + US-246 integration)
stories.intent: Sprint 20 shipped US-242 (idle-poll escalation), US-244 (non-blocking BT-connect), and US-246 (defer-INSERT). Each has unit coverage but no integration test exercises the full key-on -> idle-poll -> escalation -> drive_start -> sensor-arrival -> drive_summary INSERT -> drive_end -> sync lifecycle. Drive 6 (post-Sprint-21 action item) will exercise this. This synthetic test is the ground-truth gate.

stories.title: Sprint 18 retroactive integration tests for US-216 + US-228 (per feedback_runtime_validation_required)
stories.intent: Sprint 18's US-216 and US-228 both shipped passes:true but failed silently in production for weeks. Per feedback_runtime_validation_required.md (rule born from this lesson), synthetic tests should have been strong enough to surface the bugs. This story closes the retro loop: write tests that WOULD have caught those bugs, prove they fail against pre-Sprint-19 code, document them as exemplars. Doubles as refresher on what 'tightly mock at hardware-signal level' means.

tests/pi/obdii/test_us228_retro_pre_sprint19_failure_mode.py (NEW -- analogous for US-228; mocks Drives 3/4/5 trajectory; demonstrates Sprint 19 US-236 inverted-assertion path)
